text:
Market today: Tech sentiment is a bit shaky today, with some AI stock concerns, but overall market support from massive corporate buybacks could provide a floor. This tug-of-war directly impacts your concentrated tech/chip book.

Your holdings: SOXL (-24.38%) is a SELL, and SPAL (-20.64%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged ETF also needing attention. Both are leveraged, and margin amplifies drops, so these drawdowns hurt extra.

Watch: Keep a close eye on broader tech sentiment, especially around AI stocks, as this directly affects your core holdings. Also, monitor your leveraged positions closely for stop-loss breaches.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing risk on your leveraged and underperforming positions.
plays: SOXL: Trim this leveraged position. It's a "SELL" at -24.38%; respect the default stop at 200 or adjust it tighter to manage risk.
SPAL: This new, leveraged ETF is already down -20.64%. Consider trimming or tightening its stop at 21.38 to protect capital.
No new buys today - hold and watch. The market signals for new tech buys aren't strong enough to act on the scanner watchlist right now.
